evals.prompt: Add JSDoc documentation to our NestJS service file. It has 8 methods including CRUD operations, a search method with complex filtering, and a batch import method. Document parameters, return types, throws, and examples.
evals.expected_output: JSDoc comments on all 8 methods with @param, @returns, @throws, and @example tags

assertions.name: all_methods_documented
assertions.description: JSDoc on all 8 public methods

assertions.name: param_tags
assertions.description: @param with type and description for each parameter

assertions.name: returns_tags
assertions.description: @returns with type and description

assertions.name: throws_tags
assertions.description: @throws for methods that throw exceptions

assertions.name: examples
assertions.description: @example with usage examples for complex methods

evals.prompt: Add Python docstrings to our Django service using Google style. The service has methods for user management (create, update, delete, search, bulk_import). Include type hints and examples.
evals.expected_output: Google-style docstrings on all methods with Args, Returns, Raises, and Examples sections

assertions.name: google_style
assertions.description: Uses Google-style docstring format

assertions.name: args_section
assertions.description: Args section with parameter names, types, and descriptions

assertions.name: returns_section
assertions.description: Returns section with type and description

assertions.name: raises_section
assertions.description: Raises section for methods that raise exceptions

assertions.name: type_hints
assertions.description: Function signatures include type hints

evals.prompt: Document our React component library. Each component needs: a description of what it does, its props table (name, type, required, default, description), and usage examples. Components: Button, Input, Modal, Card, Table.
evals.expected_output: Component documentation with prop tables and usage examples for all 5 components

assertions.name: five_components
assertions.description: Documentation for Button, Input, Modal, Card, Table

assertions.name: props_table
assertions.description: Props listed with name, type, required flag, default value, description

assertions.name: usage_examples
assertions.description: JSX usage examples for each component

assertions.name: component_description
assertions.description: Clear description of what each component does and when to use it

assertions.name: variant_examples
assertions.description: Examples showing different variants/states

evals.prompt: Our codebase has TODO and FIXME comments scattered across 50 files. Generate a report of all TODOs with: file path, line number, the comment text, and whether it references a ticket number.
evals.expected_output: Structured TODO report with file:line references and ticket tracking

assertions.name: scans_codebase
assertions.description: Greps for TODO and FIXME across the codebase

assertions.name: file_line_refs
assertions.description: Each TODO listed with file path and line number

assertions.name: ticket_detection
assertions.description: Identifies TODOs that reference ticket numbers vs orphaned TODOs

assertions.name: priority_suggestion
assertions.description: Suggests prioritizing TODOs without tickets

assertions.name: structured_output
assertions.description: Output in a scannable table or list format

evals.prompt: Add inline comments to a complex algorithm. The function processes a graph to find the shortest path between two nodes using Dijkstra's algorithm, but the code has no comments and uses single-letter variable names.
evals.expected_output: Comments explaining the algorithm steps, renamed variables for clarity, and complexity annotation

assertions.name: algorithm_explanation
assertions.description: Comment block explaining the overall algorithm approach

assertions.name: step_comments
assertions.description: Inline comments on each major step (init, process, extract path)

assertions.name: variable_clarity
assertions.description: Suggests or applies variable renaming (d→distances, q→queue)

assertions.name: complexity_annotation
assertions.description: Time and space complexity noted (O(V log V + E))

assertions.name: not_over_commented
assertions.description: Comments explain why, not what (no 'increment i by 1')
